items.title: Box breathing (2 minutes)
items.text: Try 4-4-4-4 breathing: inhale 4, hold 4, exhale 4, hold 4. Repeat for 2 minutes. If it feels too intense, shorten the hold.

items.title: 5-4-3-2-1 grounding
items.text: Name 5 things you see, 4 you feel, 3 you hear, 2 you smell, 1 you taste. Go slowly and describe each item.

items.title: Simple wind-down routine
items.text: For tonight: dim lights, no screens for 20–30 minutes, slow breathing 2 minutes, and write worries on paper (then set it aside). Aim for “calmer”, not perfect.

items.title: When exams feel overwhelming
items.text: Pick one tiny next step for 15–20 minutes (not the whole syllabus). After one block, take a 3-minute break and choose the next single step.

items.title: Reaching out safely
items.text: If you can, message one trusted person: “I’m having a hard day—can you stay with me for a bit?” Small contact can reduce intensity even if the problem isn’t solved yet.

items.title: Getting support
items.text: If this has been persistent, severe, or impacting daily life, talking to a licensed professional can help. If you share your country, I can suggest appropriate helpline and next-step options.
